description: TraceRTM MCP Server Configuration - Supports both STDIO and HTTP modes

tracertm-stdio.description: TraceRTM MCP Server via STDIO (default mode)

tracertm-http.description: TraceRTM MCP Server via HTTP (requires running FastAPI server)

http-with-bearer-token.description: HTTP mode with Bearer token authentication

notes.stdio_mode: STDIO mode is the default and recommended for local Claude Desktop usage. The server runs as a subprocess and communicates via stdin/stdout.
notes.http_mode: HTTP mode requires a running FastAPI server (uvicorn src.tracertm.api.main:app). Useful for remote servers or web applications.
notes.authentication: Supports static API keys, OAuth, database-based auth, and bearer tokens. Configure via TRACERTM_MCP_AUTH_MODE environment variable.
notes.switching_modes: To switch between STDIO and HTTP, update the active server name in Claude Desktop settings or modify this config file.
notes.logging: Set TRACERTM_MCP_LOG_LEVEL to DEBUG for detailed logs. Logs are written to stdout (STDIO) or server logs (HTTP).
notes.performance: HTTP mode adds network latency but allows multiple clients. STDIO mode has lower latency but supports single client only.
